mechanism_narrative: A persistently high unemployment rate (UNRATE) reflects significant slack in the labor market and weak aggregate demand, leading to lower corporate earnings expectations and negative forward equity returns as the recovery stalls.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.7% is historically elevated, indicating a weak labor market.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the 3-month change in UNRATE is positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is near the zero lower bound, the perceived inability of the central bank to provide further accommodation makes equities more vulnerable to negative shocks, leading to negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% is effectively at the zero lower bound.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not statistically lower than baseline when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a broad-based contraction in consumer demand for both durable and non-durable goods. This joint signal of weakness leads to downward revisions in corporate earnings and negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST value of 575.0 is historically low.
failure_condition: The interaction term between HOUST and RSAFS changes in a regression predicting 60-day returns is not statistically significant with a negative coefficient (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recessionary environment characterized by high unemployment, accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) is a leading indicator of economic recovery and improving corporate profitability, driving positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is high at 9.7%, defining the recovery regime.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is above 7%, the correlation between INDPRO 3-month changes and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is negative or statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with stagnant or falling industrial production (INDPRO) creates a stagflationary environment. This pressures corporate margins and forces the central bank into a difficult trade-off, increasing risk premia and leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The model's hit rate does not improve when adding the CPIAUCSL-INDPRO interaction term compared to models with only the individual series.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) after a major downturn signals a durable labor market recovery. This boosts consumer confidence and spending, leading to higher expected corporate earnings and positive equity returns over a short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS value of 129750.0 is well below typical pre-crisis levels.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are not positively correlated with the acceleration of PAYEMS (3m change - 1m change) during periods following a >5% drop in PAYEMS.

mechanism_narrative: The rate of change in real GDP (GDPC1) is a direct measure of aggregate economic activity. Positive changes signal economic expansion, which translates into higher corporate revenues and profits, driving positive equity returns over a medium-term horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 is the broadest measure of economic output.
failure_condition: The correlation between quarterly changes in GDPC1 and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is not statistically different from zero over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-interest-rate environment, falling unemployment (UNRATE) signals incipient wage pressures and future inflation, prompting expectations of monetary tightening. This discounting of future rate hikes leads to negative equity returns, even as the labor market improves.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 2%, periods of falling unemployment are not followed by statistically significant negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
